page: Vultr voc-c-4c-8gb-75s-amd is an instance in the Optimized Cloud Compute family. It features 4 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and is bundled with 75 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. No GPU is included in this configuration.

Benchmark results show strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 10% and bzip3 single-threaded decompression also reaching the top 10%. However, multi-core and multi-threaded performance is more moderate due to the shared CPU allocation. Stress-ng multi-core performance and PassMark CPU scores fall into the average range, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and static web serving throughput are relatively weak, placing in the bottom 25%.

Memory performance is balanced, with write bandwidth for large blocks reaching a strong 116.58 GB/sec, while read bandwidth remains average. The instance is equipped with advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and shared CPU allocation, this instance offers a cost-effective entry point for compute-focused workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated core performance. It is suitable for single-threaded processing, lightweight containerized applications, and basic development environments.
description: Vultr voc-c-4c-8gb-75s-amd is an AMD EPYC Genoa-based instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 4 shared vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM. This x86_64 server is bundled with 75 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and lacks a dedicated GPU. Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core and single-threaded bzip3 decompression scores placing in the top 10%. Conversely, multi-threaded performance and database operations are weaker, reflecting the shared CPU allocation. Memory write bandwidth is strong at 116.58 GB/sec, while read bandwidth and LLM inference speeds for larger models remain average to poor. This instance provides a cost-effective option for workloads prioritizing single-threaded CPU speed over sustained multi-core execution, making it suitable for lightweight web serving, development environments, and basic utility applications.
og_description: Vultr voc-c-4c-8gb-75s-amd is an AMD EPYC Genoa instance with 4 shared vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 75 GB NVMe SSD storage, offering strong single-core performance alongside cost-effective shared compute resources.
meta_description: Vultr voc-c-4c-8gb-75s-amd features 4 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 75 GB NVMe storage, delivering strong single-core performance.
tagline: A cost-effective shared-core instance pairing strong single-threaded AMD Genoa performance with fast local NVMe SSD storage for lightweight compute workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 4 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs on x86_64 architecture
Includes 8.0 GB of system memory and 75 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded decompression performance
Weaker multi-threaded scaling and database performance due to shared CPU allocation
Best suited for lightweight web serving, development, and single-threaded tasks
